channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Pussy Riot joins anti-Russia smoke bomb protest at Venice Biennale
item.description: Among those in the crowed were members of the Russian punk group Pussy Riot, who attended protests against the country's participation in the Italian art show.

media:group.media:description: The protests were prompted by Russia's participation in the Italian art show.

item.title: Iran reviews US proposal as Trump says war could 'be at an end'
item.description: Iran reviews a new peace-deal proposal from the US, which President Donald Trump says could end the war, but an Iranian official describes it as an "American wish list".
item.dc:creator: Brad Ryan

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump said bombing would resume at a "much higher level" if Iran did not agree to a deal.

item.title: Ted Turner, pioneering founder of CNN, dead at 87
item.description: Turner revolutionised television news when he launched the world's first 24-hour news channel in 1980.

media:group.media:description: Ted Turner's decision to found CNN was initially mocked, but the network would reshape the way audiences consumed news.

item.title: Australian government 'deciding factor' in departure of ISIS-linked families from Syria
item.description: The Syrian government says Australia was the ultimate decision-maker in the delay of a group of four women and nine children with links to Islamic State leaving Syria for Australia.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Rollason

media:group.media:description: A larger group of Australians attempted to leave the Al Roj refugee camp for home in February.

item.title: Murder trial hears couple 'looked madly in love' before violent death
item.description: Braden Jentian's defence lawyer has told jurors his partner was killed by "something or someone else" before she was found dead on a Katherine street in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Barrett Peters

media:group.media:description: Rebecca Everitt told the jury the couple spent several hours drinking at bars around Katherine on the day she died.

item.title: Olympic stadium at Victoria Park cleared by environmental department
item.description: The proposal for a new stadium to be built at Victoria Park and an aquatic centre in Spring Hill has been declared "not a controlled action" by the federal environmental department.
item.dc:creator: Liana Walker

media:group.media:description: The stadium for the 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games at Victoria Park has been cleared by the environmental department.

item.title: 'Terrible' cockroach infestation driving residents out of Perth housing block
item.description: Residents are seeking to break their leases as a cockroach infestation at an inner-west public housing unit spreads.
item.dc:creator: Kenith Png

media:group.media:description: The infestation started at a public housing unit.

item.title: Canary Islands leader doesn't want hantavirus-hit cruise ship to dock
item.description: A luxury cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak and marooned for days off the coast of Cape Verde with close to 150 people on board is on its way to Spain's Canary Islands, where a regional leader says it is not welcome.

media:group.media:description: Health workers in protective gear evacuate patients from the MV Hondius cruise ship at a port in Praia, Cape Verde on Wednesday.

item.title: Ministers insist 'no conflict of interest' before declaring relationship
item.description: Queensland ministers Tim Mander and Amanda Camm have fielded questions about their relationship and timeline.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Tim Mander and Amanda Camm have faced more questions about their relationship.

item.title: Mother's warning after cake decorating dust leaves toddler in coma
item.description: The mother of a Queensland toddler who is in an induced coma after inhaling a dust used for decorating cakes is calling for similar products to be pulled from shelves.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Williams and Jordan Bissell

media:group.media:description: Dustin Wildman is in an induced coma after inhaling dust used to decorate cakes.

item.title: Exercise physiologists demand an end to 'discriminatory' health tax
item.description: The peak body for exercise physiologists says its members are being discriminated against with "unfair" taxes that other health services do not have to pay.
item.dc:creator: Kenji Sato

media:group.media:description: Tim Dogue says the GST on exercise physiology services is unfair and discriminatory.

item.title: Cries of censorship as Sydney council bars local paper from meetings
item.description: The council has barred reporters it says are "rage baiting" the community and harassing staff, with some councillors accusing a 140-year-old newspaper of not being what it purports to be.
item.dc:creator: Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: Labor councillor Amanda Kotlash says the Gazette is not "the press".

item.title: Perth blanketed in thick smoke haze, caution urged
item.description: Perth has been blanketed in a thick smoke haze, the result of a prescribed burn off in the city's eastern fringes.
item.dc:creator: Greig Johnston and Jasmine Hughes

media:group.media:description: Smoke sets on Perth's CBD on Wednesday afternoon.

item.title: 'Personal information' of Australian students potentially stolen in Canvas data hack
item.description: The international learning management software Canvas, used by various universities and schools, was hacked on Saturday. One of the facilities impacted says some of its data has been accessed by a "criminal third party".
item.dc:creator: Scout Wallen and Monty Jacka

media:group.media:description: Canvas is a learning management software used by educational facilities across the world.

item.title: Fed Square scraps World Cup screenings citing 'antisocial' behaviour
item.description: The Melbourne Arts Precinct says the past actions of a "small number of people" at screenings in previous years has prompted the decision to not show matches this year.

media:group.media:description: Fans lit flares after an equalising goal from Sam Kerr in 2023, filling the square with smoke.

item.title: Prosecution appeals sentence for driver who caused five road deaths
item.description: Prosecutors tell the Court of Appeal that "something has gone wrong" in sentencing a driver who caused one of Victoria's deadliest crashes.
item.dc:creator: Charmaine Manuel

media:group.media:description: Christopher Dillon Joannidis (left), with Eleanor Theebom, was sentenced to serve at least three years in jail.

item.title: Jury hears man recorded saying he was going to 'kill him' days before fatal car crash
item.description: Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe has pleaded not guilty to the murder of Dale McKenna, three years after a fatal crash that followed an alleged car chase on a rural Queensland road.
item.dc:creator: Grace Nakamura and Cindy Wockner

media:group.media:description: Dale Aaron McKenna was 22 years old when he died.

item.title: Couple sues Monash IVF for negligence over embryo mix-up
item.description: A couple is suing fertility giant Monash IVF for negligence over an embryo mix-up at its Brisbane clinic discovered last year.

media:group.media:description: Monash IVF transferred the wrong embryo to a woman at its Brisbane clinic in 2023.

item.title: Iran war a key driver of increased living costs, new ABS data finds
item.description: Cost-of-living pressures have increased for all households in Australia, partly due to the war in the Middle East, according to the Australian Bureau of Statistics.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Steedman

media:group.media:description: All household types saw an increase in transport costs of at least 2 per cent in the first quarter.

item.title: Driver dies in single-vehicle crash at Canberra underpass
item.description: Australian Federal Police officers were first on scene and tried to save the man.

media:group.media:description: ACT Policing's Detective Sergeant William Stevenson said he visited the man's family to let them know about the fatality.

item.title: One Nation corflutes backing Ben Roberts-Smith spark legal warning
item.description: One Nation election advertising stating the party's support for Ben Roberts-Smith, who is charged with carrying out war crimes in Afghanistan, has legal experts worried.
item.dc:creator: Jess Scully

media:group.media:description: Signs depicting Ben Roberts-Smith have been used in the Farrer by-election campaign.

item.title: 'I don't owe LIV anything': SA premier on struggling golf league's Adelaide future
item.description: As LIV Golf tries to plug the funding hole left by Saudi Arabia, Peter Malinauskas says he would welcome the tour back to Adelaide, but only "if it stacks up for us".
item.dc:creator: Thomas Kelsall

media:group.media:description: Peter Malinauskas at the announcement last year that LIV Golf would stay in Adelaide until 2031.

item.title: Son sentenced to 22 years' jail over arson murder of his mother
item.description: Shannon James Doble will remain behind bars until at least 2040 for the "callous" attack on his 65-year-old mother, who died after receiving burns to 90 per cent of her body.
item.dc:creator: Melinda Hayter

media:group.media:description: Shannon James Doble pleaded guilty to his mother's murder in September last year.

item.title: Police officers accused of alleged assault of mentally ill prisoner
item.description: Two Queensland police officers are contesting the charges that relate to a 2024 incident at a regional watch house, arguing that investigators added things to the alleged victim's statement that he never said.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: Jeffrey Hunter KC (left), defence barrister for Senior Constable Ulrich Weiss, and David Jones KC (second from left), counsel for Senior Constable Andrew Couchman, leave the Cairns courthouse with solicitors.

item.title: Snow, rain, icy winds forecast as polar blast hits south-east Australia
item.description: A wintry mix of snow, icy temperatures, and showers is set to hit Australia's south-east, bringing a sharp end to the recent run of warm autumn weather.
item.dc:creator: Tyne Logan

media:group.media:description: There have already been several instances of snow this year in Tasmania, but this is likely to be the lowest snow for many.
